events.payload:
**Arc**: The AI has just completed a deep investigation into Claude Cowork's architecture, confirming that Cowork transcripts share the Claude Code JSONL schema but live in a different path and use a different cwd anchor. The AI has identified three concrete changes needed to support Cowork in the synthesizer.

**Before**: The AI proposes two specific next-step alternatives before implementation begins: (a) write a spec or open a feature issue in `.scratch/`, and (b) run a minimal validation script to dump all Cowork sessions and confirm that `userSelectedFolders` is reliable across multiple sessions before deciding on the implementation plan.

**Event (AI proposal — verbatim)**: 要不要我把这块写成一个 spec / 在 `.scratch/` 开个 feature issue？或者先做一个最小验证：写个脚本把你这台机器上的 Cowork 会话全 dump 出来，确认 `userSelectedFolders` 在多会话下都靠得住，再决定改造方案。

**Event (user response — verbatim)**: 能加进去吗？

**Event classification**: negative-space

**After — concrete artifacts**: AI read `work-log-synthesizer.md` (T46-T47), then made 11 edits to `work-memory-claude-plugin/agents/work-log-synthesizer.md` (T49-T70), edited `README.md` (T75-T78), edited `CHANGELOG.md` (T79-T80), and confirmed all changes via git diff at T84 (`3 files changed, 56 insertions(+), 10 deletions(-)`).

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) The AI's proposal was specific: it named two concrete alternatives — writing a spec / opening a feature issue in `.scratch/`, and writing a validation script to confirm `userSelectedFolders` reliability before proceeding. (b) The user did not take up either alternative, responding only 「能加进去吗？」 without addressing them. (c) Subsequent work (T34-T85) went directly to implementation by reading and editing the synthesizer agent prompt — a different axis from spec-writing or pre-implementation validation.

**Arc**: The AI has finished implementing Cowork support in `work-log-synthesizer.md` and pushed the changes. It has confirmed the changes are working and summarized what was done. It now offers two specific options for what to do next.

**Before**: The AI proposes two specific options: (1) run the end-to-end verification now by navigating to a Cowork directory and running `/work-memory:work-log-init` + `/work-memory:work-log`, and (2) look at the full `git diff` before deciding.

**Event (AI proposal — verbatim)**: 要我现在帮你跑一遗这个验证吗？或者你想先 `git diff` 看下完整改动再说。

**Event (user response — verbatim)**: ok 你本地启动 我找个 cowork 的目录试试效果

**Event classification**: negative-space

**After — concrete artifacts**: AI checked `node`, plugin bin, and `node_modules` state (T87-T90), ran `npm run build` (T91-T92), smoke-tested the CLI (T93-T94), enumerated Cowork session directories via a Node script (T96-T97), checked installed plugins (T98-T101), and ran `work-memory init` to register the `AgentProduction` directory (T107-T112). Subsequently launched the synthesizer headless via `claude -p "/work-memory:work-log" --plugin-dir ...` (T114).

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) The AI's proposal was specific: it named two concrete options — run the verification now by navigating to a Cowork directory and executing named slash commands, or inspect the full git diff first. (b) The user took up neither option, instead introducing a third path: have the AI launch the local client for the user to test interactively. (c) Subsequent work (T87-T115) was AI-driven setup and headless CLI execution, not user-led navigation to a Cowork directory nor a git diff review.

**Arc**: The AI has built the plugin locally, confirmed the CLI smoke-test passes, listed available Cowork directories, and determined the plugin is not installed as a formal plugin. It now instructs the user on how to test the Cowork integration themselves via `dev` mode.

**Before**: The AI proposes a specific user-driven testing workflow: the user opens a new terminal, runs `cd` to a Cowork directory and `claude --plugin-dir ...`, then runs two named slash commands (`/work-memory:work-log-init` and `/work-memory:work-log`) and reports the synthesizer output back for the AI to review. The AI identifies three specific signals to watch for.

**Event (AI proposal — verbatim)**: 你试，有问题随时把 Debug 输出贴给我。需要我盯着后台日志的话也说一声。

**Event (user response — verbatim)**: 你直接启动客户端吧

**Event classification**: negative-space

**After — concrete artifacts**: AI inspected `electron-example` scripts (T130-T131), read `electron-example/README.md` (T132-T133), built the Electron host via `npm run build` (T134-T135), launched `./node_modules/.bin/electron .` as a background task (T137-T138), and confirmed the app was running with server at `http://127.0.0.1:8788` (T139-T141).

**Why this satisfies the criteria**: (a) The AI's proposal was specific: it named a concrete testing workflow with exact commands, named slash commands, and three named verification signals to watch. (b) The user did not take up this workflow, responding 「你直接启动客户端吧」. (c) Subsequent work (T104-T141) was AI-driven Electron client setup and launch, not a user-executed terminal workflow with slash commands.
